summary: Part-to-whole donut chart with a center aggregate, responsive fill sizing, local slice tooltip feedback, and externally controlled legend highlighting.
useWhen: Users need to understand a small part-to-whole breakdown with a prominent total or status in the center.
A standalone donut needs pointer and keyboard-focus value feedback through a chart-owned data-viz tooltip.
A donut and visible ChartLegend should cross-highlight by matching stable labels.
avoidWhen: The data is a trend, time sequence, or independent magnitude comparison.
The category count creates slices too small to understand reliably.
Hover is intended to select, filter, or navigate rather than temporarily highlight data.

alternatives.when: The product needs the canonical card, filter action, visible legend, and built-in hover synchronization.
alternatives.reason: The dedicated card owns that complete composition and suppresses redundant slice tooltips.

alternatives.when: Users need to compare independent category magnitudes.
alternatives.reason: Bars make category magnitude differences easier to compare.

accessibility: Every non-empty slice remains keyboard focusable and exposes its label and value without depending on the visual tooltip.
Use a visible legend when the full set of labels, values, or percentages must remain continuously available.
Provide localized no-data text for the neutral empty ring.
Center text is a visual summary and must not be the only source of essential category detail.
states: Genuine local pointer or keyboard focus may show a chart-owned tooltip; externally synchronized highlight never opens it.
When a visible legend already owns label and value detail, suppress the redundant donut tooltip. CardDataVizDonut does this automatically.
Local hover and external highlight remain separate while both may dim non-matching slices.
Zero or negative totals render a neutral empty ring without interactive slices.
Center value and caption truncate when they exceed the safe inner-circle measure.
responsiveBehavior: Prefer container-fill sizing inside data-viz cards and explicit sizing only in layouts that own a stable diameter.
Fill sizing remains centered and constrained to the shared donut size band.
The owner adapts category count and legend placement for available space.

customElements: Assign data as a JavaScript property and wire slice-hover detail to a sibling legend only when not using CardDataVizDonut.
react: Keep synchronized highlight controlled by the composition and disable slice tooltips when a sibling legend continuously shows the same details.
angular: Keep synchronized highlight controlled by the composition and disable slice tooltips when a sibling legend continuously shows the same details.
